# Freelance Tools — Free Offline Tools & Guides for Freelancers

> Free, single-file, offline-first tools for freelancers: invoicing, time tracking, CRM,
> expenses, contracts and quotes. No login, no subscription, your data stays in your browser.
> Paid Pro versions are one-time purchases on Gumroad. Site: https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/

## Free tools (run in the browser, nothing to install)
- [Free Invoice Generator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-invoice-generator/): Offline single-file invoice & quote maker
- [Free Time Tracker](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-time-tracker/): Billable-hours timesheet, runs in your browser
- [Free Freelance CRM](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-freelance-crm/): Lead & client sales-pipeline tracker
- [Free Expense Tracker](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-expense-tracker/): Income, expenses & tax set-aside tracker
- [Free Freelance Contract](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-freelance-contract/): Proposal & service-contract maker

## Free calculators & utilities
- [Freelance Rate Calculator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/freelance-rate-calculator/): Find/check your hourly rate from an income goal
- [Invoice Late-Fee Calculator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/invoice-late-fee-calculator/): Compute overdue fees + reminder text
- [Hourly to Salary Calculator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/hourly-to-salary-calculator/): Convert hourly <-> annual both ways
- [Project Quote Builder](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/project-quote-builder/): Line-item quote with discount/tax, print to PDF
- [Invoice Due-Date Calculator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/invoice-due-date-calculator/): Net 7/15/30 due dates + early-pay discount
- [Freelance Tax Estimator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/freelance-tax-estimator/): Quarterly/monthly set-aside estimate
- [Invoice Number Generator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/invoice-number-generator/): Auto-incrementing invoice numbers
- [Freelance Project Checklist](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/freelance-project-checklist/): Pre-built 16-item project checklist

## Guides
- [What to Do When a Client Won't Pay Your Invoice](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/what-to-do-when-a-client-wont-pay/): A calm, step-by-step process for freelancers when a client won
- [Tracking Business Expenses as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/tracking-business-expenses-as-a-freelancer/): A simple system for tracking freelance business expenses: what counts, how to capture receipts, and how good records cut your tax bill legitimately.
- [Time Tracking Methods for Freelancers: Timer, Manual, Blocks, and Pomodoro](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/time-tracking-methods-for-freelancers/): Compare time tracking methods for freelancers, including timers, manual logs, time-blocking, Pomodoro, billable vs non-billable time, invoices, and mistakes to avoid.
- [Time Blocking for Freelancers: A Practical Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/time-blocking-for-freelancers/): How freelancers can use time blocking to protect deep work, batch admin, and bill more accurately &mdash; with a simple setup you
- [Setting Aside for Health Insurance as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/setting-aside-for-health-insurance-freelance/): How freelancers can budget for health insurance: why it belongs in your rate, how to set money aside consistently, and how to treat it as a business cost.
- [Should Freelancers Charge Sales Tax? A Plain Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/should-freelancers-charge-sales-tax/): When freelancers need to charge sales tax or VAT, how thresholds and location work, and how to track it &mdash; general info, not tax advice.
- [Separating Business and Personal Finances as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/separating-business-and-personal-finances/): Why and how freelancers should separate business and personal finances: accounts, a pay-yourself routine, and cleaner books at tax time.
- [Scope Creep: Scripts to Push Back Professionally](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/scope-creep-scripts/): Ready-to-use scripts for handling scope creep as a freelancer: how to say no to extra work, reset expectations, and turn requests into paid change orders.
- [Partial Payments: How to Record Them Without Confusion](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/recording-partial-payments/): How freelancers should record partial payments: tracking what is paid and outstanding, applying part-payments to invoices, and keeping a clean balance per client.
- [Retainer vs Hourly: Which Should You Offer Clients?](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/retainer-vs-hourly-pricing/): A clear comparison of retainer and hourly pricing for freelancers: the pros and cons of each, when to use which, and how to choose for a given client.
- [How to Write a Payment Reminder Email as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/payment-reminder-email-examples-for-freelancers/): A practical guide to writing payment reminder emails that are firm, clear, and professional without sounding dramatic or apologetic.
- [Offline Freelance Tools vs Cloud Apps: A Practical Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/offline-vs-cloud-freelance-tools/): Compare offline freelance tools with cloud apps for privacy, data ownership, subscriptions, reliability, collaboration, and the long-term cost of each.
- [Net 30 vs Due on Receipt: Which Payment Term to Use](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/net-30-vs-due-on-receipt/): Net 30 vs due on receipt explained for freelancers: how each term affects cash flow, client expectations, and how to pick the right one per job.
- [New Client Onboarding Checklist for Freelancers](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/new-client-onboarding-checklist/): A repeatable freelance onboarding checklist: contract, deposit, kickoff questions, access, and expectations &mdash; so every project starts cleanly.
- [Multi-Currency Invoicing for Freelancers: A Practical Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/multi-currency-invoicing-for-freelancers/): How to invoice international clients in different currencies: which currency to bill, handling exchange rates and fees, and keeping clean records across currencies.
- [Late Payment Interest and Your Rights as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/late-payment-interest-and-rights/): How late-payment interest and fees work for freelancers: what you can charge, how to state it, and how to enforce it without losing the client.
- [Milestone Billing vs Deposit Billing for Freelancers](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/milestone-vs-deposit-billing/): Milestone vs deposit billing compared: how each protects cash flow on freelance projects, when to use which, and how to structure the payments.
- [A Mid-Year Financial Review for Freelancers](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/june-freelance-financial-review/): A practical June financial review for freelancers covering revenue, expenses, taxes, cash runway, pricing, and the second half of the year.
- [Kill Fees and Cancellation Clauses for Freelancers](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/kill-fees-and-cancellation-clauses/): What a kill fee is, why freelancers need one, and how to write fair cancellation clauses that protect you when a project is cancelled mid-stream.
- [Invoice Numbering Systems for Freelancers, Explained](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/invoice-numbering-systems/): How to number freelance invoices the right way: why sequence matters, common numbering schemes, what to avoid, and how to stay consistent as you grow.
- [How to Write a Winning Freelance Project Proposal](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-write-a-winning-project-proposal/): A practical structure for freelance proposals that win work: framing the problem, scoping clearly, pricing options, and closing without pressure.
- [How to Write a Statement of Work (SOW) as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-write-a-statement-of-work/): A practical guide to writing a freelance statement of work: what to include, how it differs from a contract, and how a clear SOW prevents scope disputes.
- [How to Write a Freelance Invoice: Your Step-by-Step Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-write-a-freelance-invoice/): Learn how to write a freelance invoice with the right fields, payment terms, tax details, and follow-up steps so clients can pay you promptly and reliably.
- [How to Write a Freelance Contract (Plain-English Guide)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-write-a-freelance-contract/): Learn how to write a freelance contract with clear scope, payment terms, revisions, ownership, cancellation, kill fee, signatures, and common mistakes to avoid.
- [How to Track Subcontractor Costs as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-track-subcontractor-costs/): How to track money paid to subcontractors: separating their costs from your profit, recording payments, handling markup, and keeping clean records for tax time.
- [How to Track Billable Hours as a Freelancer: Simple Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-track-billable-hours/): Learn how to track billable hours, separate non-billable work, choose fair rounding rules, measure your effective rate, and create accurate client invoices.
- [How to Read a Freelance Profit and Loss Statement](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-read-a-freelance-profit-and-loss/): A plain-English guide to reading a freelance P&L: what each section means, how revenue becomes profit, and what the numbers tell you about your business.
- [How to Raise Your Rates Without Losing Clients](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-raise-rates-without-losing-clients/): A practical guide to raising freelance rates: when to do it, how to communicate an increase, who to raise first, and how to keep good clients through the change.
- [How to Present Pricing Tiers in a Freelance Proposal](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-present-pricing-tiers/): How to structure and present pricing tiers as a freelancer: why three options convert better, how to design each tier, and how to guide clients to a choice.
- [How to Manage Multiple Freelance Clients Without Dropping Leads](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-manage-multiple-freelance-clients/): Learn how to manage multiple freelance clients with a lightweight CRM, clear pipeline stages, follow-up cadence, client prioritization, and simple weekly review.
- [How to Organize Freelance Finances Without Making It Complicated](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-organize-freelance-finances/): Learn how to organize freelance finances with separate business money, income and expense tracking, tax set-asides, a monthly routine, and clean records.
- [How to Invoice International Clients as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-invoice-international-clients/): A practical guide to invoicing international clients: currency, payment rails, VAT/tax notes, and fields that prevent disputes and slow payments.
- [How to Invoice for a Deposit (Without Confusing the Client)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-invoice-for-a-deposit/): A practical guide to invoicing a deposit as a freelancer: how to word it, what percentage to ask for, how to apply it to the final bill, and what to track.
- [How to Handle a Chargeback as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-handle-a-chargeback-freelance/): What to do when a client files a chargeback: how chargebacks work, how to respond with evidence, how to prevent them, and how to protect your freelance income.
- [How to Follow Up on an Unpaid Invoice Without Sounding Pushy](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-follow-up-on-an-unpaid-invoice/): Learn how to follow up on an unpaid invoice with a calm sequence before the due date, day 1 overdue, day 7, day 14, late fees, records, and escalation.
- [How to Fire a Freelance Client Professionally](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-fire-a-freelance-client-professionally/): When and how to end a freelance client relationship cleanly: the warning signs, the conversation, the handoff, and protecting your reputation.
- [How to Estimate a Freelance Project Accurately](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-estimate-a-project-accurately/): A method for estimating freelance projects accurately: break down tasks, use your real time data, pad for unknowns, and avoid the planning fallacy.
- [How to End a Retainer Gracefully](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-end-a-retainer-gracefully/): How to end a freelance retainer professionally: when to do it, how to give notice, handling the handover, and protecting the relationship and your reputation.
- [How to Ask for a Deposit as a Freelancer (Without Losing the Client)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-ask-for-a-deposit-as-a-freelancer/): How much deposit to ask for, when to request it, the exact wording to use, and how to split a project into deposit and milestone payments so you are never working unpaid.
- [How Much Should Freelancers Set Aside for Taxes Each Year?](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-much-to-set-aside-for-taxes-freelance/): Learn how much to set aside for taxes freelance income may create, how the 25-30% habit works, and how to track expenses and estimated payments each year.
- [How Much Should a Freelancer Save? Emergency Fund & Runway](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-much-should-a-freelancer-save/): How big a freelance emergency fund should be, how to calculate your runway in months, how to find your monthly break-even, and how to build a buffer against slow periods and late payments.
- [How Much Runway Before Going Full-Time Freelance](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-much-runway-before-going-full-time/): How to calculate the financial runway you need before quitting your job to freelance full-time, plus the income signals that say you
- [How Much Do PayPal and Stripe Take From Freelancers? (2026)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-much-do-paypal-stripe-take-from-freelancers/): A clear 2026 breakdown of PayPal, Stripe, Wise and Payoneer fees for freelancers, how to calculate what you actually keep, and how to price invoices so fees do not eat your rate.
- [Hourly vs Fixed vs Value-Based Pricing for Freelancers](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/hourly-vs-fixed-vs-value-based-pricing/): Hourly, fixed, and value-based pricing compared for freelancers: the trade-offs of each model and how to choose the right one for a given project.
- [How to Give a Discount Without Devaluing Your Work](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/giving-a-discount-without-devaluing-your-work/): How freelancers can offer discounts without cheapening their rates: trade scope for price, use conditional discounts, and protect your future pricing.
- [A Freelancer's Slow Season Survival Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-slow-season-cash-flow-plan/): A practical slow season plan for freelancers: protect cash, adjust expenses, follow up with warm leads, and avoid panic discounts.
- [Freelance Year-End Financial Checklist](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-year-end-financial-checklist/): A practical year-end financial checklist for freelancers: reconcile income, total expenses, check tax set-aside, chase invoices, and plan next year.
- [Freelance Sales Pipeline: A Simple System for More Leads](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-sales-pipeline/): Build a freelance sales pipeline that tracks leads, follow-ups, proposals, wins, and losses so promising client conversations do not disappear unnoticed.
- [Freelance Rush Fees: How to Price Expedited Work](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-rush-fee-pricing/): How and when to charge a freelance rush fee: why expedited work costs more, how to set the premium, and how to present it so clients accept it.
- [How to Write a Freelance Refund Policy That Protects You](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-refund-policy/): How freelancers can set a clear, fair refund policy: what to offer, what to exclude, how to word it in your contract, and how to handle refund requests calmly.
- [Freelance Retainer Agreement: What to Include Before Monthly Work Starts](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-retainer-agreement/): Learn what a freelance retainer agreement is, how hours, deliverables, and access retainers work, what clauses to include, and how to price and bill one.
- [Freelance Quarterly Estimated Taxes: How They Work](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-quarterly-estimated-taxes/): How quarterly estimated taxes work for freelancers: why they exist, how to estimate what to set aside, and how to avoid underpayment surprises.
- [Proposal vs Contract: What Freelancers Actually Need](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-proposal-vs-contract-guide/): A practical explanation of how freelance proposals and contracts differ, what belongs in each, and how to move from offer to agreement cleanly.
- [The Freelance Proposal Follow-Up Email That Works](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-proposal-follow-up-email/): How to follow up on a freelance proposal without being pushy: when to send, what to say, and templates that move a quiet prospect toward a decision.
- [Freelance Profit Margin Basics: What You Actually Keep](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-profit-margin-basics/): Understand freelance profit margin: revenue vs profit, how to calculate your real margin, why it matters more than headline rates, and how to improve it.
- [Free Freelance Invoice Template: What to Include and How to Send It](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-invoice-template/): Use this freelance invoice template guide to include the right fields, payment terms, line items, tax details, totals, and PDF sending process.
- [Freelance Invoice Payment Terms Explained: Net 15, 30, 45 and How to Get Paid Faster](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-invoice-payment-terms/): Understand freelance invoice payment terms like Net 15, Net 30, deposits, 2/10 Net 30 discounts, late fees, follow-ups, and faster payment habits.
- [How to Track Expenses as a Freelancer (Without a Spreadsheet)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-expense-tracking/): Learn freelance expense tracking without a spreadsheet, including deductible categories, receipt habits, business separation, quarterly reviews, and tax set-aside planning.
- [How to Calculate Your Day Rate as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-day-rate-calculation-guide/): A practical way to calculate a freelance day rate from income needs, available billable days, admin time, and real working capacity.
- [Do Freelancers Need a CRM?](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-crm-practical-guide/): An honest look at when freelancers need a CRM, what to track, and how to keep client follow-up organized without overbuilding a sales system.
- [Freelance Contract Red Flags to Watch For](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-contract-red-flags/): The contract clauses that quietly hurt freelancers &mdash; unlimited revisions, vague scope, IP grabs, payment on approval &mdash; and how to fix them.
- [Freelance Contract Checklist: 10 Essential Clauses You Need](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-contract-checklist/): Use this freelance contract checklist to define scope, payment, revisions, ownership, cancellation, confidentiality, liability, signatures, and disputes.
- [The Freelance Change-Order Process, Step by Step](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-change-order-process/): How to run a freelance change-order process: turning extra requests into approved, paid work without friction, and protecting your scope and margin.
- [Freelance Cash-Flow Forecasting Basics](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-cash-flow-forecasting-basics/): How to forecast freelance cash flow: predicting income and expenses, spotting shortfalls early, and smoothing lumpy freelance income with a simple forecast.
- [Estimate vs Quote vs Invoice: What Freelancers Should Send and When](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/estimate-vs-quote-vs-invoice/): Understand the difference between an estimate, quote, and invoice, when to send each document, what each should contain, and common freelance billing mistakes.
- [Common Freelance Invoicing Mistakes (and How to Fix Them)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/common-freelance-invoicing-mistakes/): The freelance invoicing mistakes that cause late payments and disputes &mdash; vague terms, missing numbers, no deposit &mdash; and simple fixes for each.
- [Client Red Flags to Spot Before You Sign](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/client-red-flags-before-you-sign/): The warning signs of a difficult freelance client that appear before the contract: vague scope, haggling, urgency, and poor communication, and how to respond.
- [Building Retainer Income as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/building-retainer-income/): How freelancers can build stable retainer income: which clients suit retainers, how to structure and price them, and how to manage the pipeline.
- [How to Bill for Meetings and Calls as a Freelancer](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/billing-for-client-meetings-and-calls/): A practical guide to billing freelance meetings and calls fairly, setting boundaries, and deciding what should be included in project pricing.
- [Are Time Tracking Apps Worth It for Freelancers?](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/are-time-tracking-apps-worth-it/): An honest look at whether freelancers need a time tracking app, what it actually buys you, and how to track time without feeding data to the cloud.

## About
All tools are single HTML files that work offline with localStorage — no servers, no tracking.
Built for freelancers who want to own their data. Pro upgrades add unlimited records, CSV
export and a tool bundle. Full list & comparisons: https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/best-free-freelance-tools/
